mainEntity.name: What is a Product Carbon Footprint (PCF)?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculates the greenhouse gas emissions of a single product along defined system boundaries – for example, from raw material extraction to the factory gate (cradle-to-gate). Methodologically, a PCF is based on standards such as ISO 14067 and the LCA fundamentals of ISO 14040/44. It makes emission drivers visible and provides a reliable basis for product decisions, customer requirements, and reporting.

mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer support companies with PCF calculation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Global Changer combines structured PCF methodology with AI-powered automation. The module analyzes product information (e.g. a URL, product description, or Bill of Materials (BOM)) and automatically identifies relevant components, processes, and materials. Our AI maps suitable emission factors from integrated databases and fills in missing data points in a traceable way. It does not operate as a black box: all mappings are displayed transparently, rated with confidence scores, and can be reviewed or replaced with primary data at any time. The calculation follows clearly defined system boundaries in accordance with ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44. Company-specific rules (such as electricity mix or materials) can be configured in the system and are applied consistently. The result: scalable, reproducible, and audit-ready PCFs – including for large portfolios with more than 10,000 products.

mainEntity.name: What data does Global Changer need for a PCF?

acceptedAnswer.text: With Global Changer, you can work with varying levels of data detail – from a quick starting point to an in-depth calculation based on primary data. For an initial PCF calculation, basic information such as a product URL or a short product description is sufficient. Based on this, our AI analyzes the available data, identifies relevant components, and fills in missing information through well-founded assumptions. If you provide a Bill of Materials (BOM), you can specify material quantities, processes, or transport details directly. Depending on the level of detail, the AI automatically fills in missing process or transport data, or uses your specific inputs. This allows for different entry levels: from a structured material list to a fully specified supply chain with primary data. The calculation always follows clearly defined system boundaries in accordance with ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44. You can start with minimal data and increase the data depth step by step – without methodological compromises.

mainEntity.name: How fast can PCFs be generated with Global Changer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Thanks to AI-powered automation, initial PCF results can be generated within 30–60 seconds. The methodological structure remains intact: system boundaries, emission factors, and calculation logic are applied consistently – especially when scaling rapidly across large portfolios. The more primary data is provided, the more reliable the result becomes.

mainEntity.name: How transparent is Global Changer's AI-powered PCF calculation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AI supports the identification of materials and processes, the mapping of suitable emission factors, and assumptions about transport – but it does not operate as a black box. All AI-generated information is clearly labeled. Emission factor mappings from databases such as ecoinvent and AGRIBALYSE are traceable, rated with confidence scores, and accompanied by reasoning. This allows you to adjust the information step by step or supplement it with primary data – fully in line with the transparency and traceability requirements of ISO 14067.

mainEntity.name: Is Global Changer's PCF module scalable for large product portfolios?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Global Changer is designed to calculate PCFs for thousands of products automatically. A single PCF can typically be generated within 30–60 seconds. This is ideal for companies with many products that share a similar structure but differ in variants. Companies can start with a few pilot products and scale flexibly to their entire portfolio. Today, customers already calculate more than 90,000 PCFs with Global Changer.

mainEntity.name: Do I have to choose between speed and data quality with AI-powered PCFs?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Our AI accelerates the most time-consuming manual steps – such as mapping suitable emission factors from recognized databases like ecoinvent and AGRIBALYSE. It does not operate as a black box: every mapping includes a source reference, confidence score, and reasoning, and can be reviewed, replaced with other emission factors from the integrated databases, or substituted with primary data at any time. The calculation is based on ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44, making every PCF reliable. Depending on your data availability, you can create PCFs ranging from an initial internal estimate to an audit-ready result for customer requests and tenders. In the future, external certification of individual PCFs will also be available.

mainEntity.name: Why aren't ChatGPT or Copilot sufficient for calculating a PCF?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generative AI tools like ChatGPT or Copilot can research information, structure text, or make rough estimates. For a reliable Product Carbon Footprint calculation, however, they fall short. An audit-ready PCF requires clearly defined system boundaries in accordance with ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44, consistent application of emission factors from integrated, versioned databases, reproducible calculation logic instead of one-off text outputs, transparent documentation of all assumptions, and comparability across products, time periods, and sites. Generative AI works probabilistically. It produces answers based on probabilities – not on the basis of a structured, rule-based accounting system. There are no fixed system boundaries, no binding methodology, and no guaranteed reproducibility of the same calculation. Additionally, company-specific rules – such as how certain electricity types are credited or which emission factors to use for specific materials – cannot be systematically configured and applied automatically. Global Changer combines AI-powered automation with fixed methodological standards, integrated emission factors, rule-based logic, and a traceable structure. The result: scalable, audit-ready PCFs – not just text-based approximations.

mainEntity.name: Can I calculate PCFs for free with Global Changer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. You can calculate two of your products for free with Global Changer to test the Product Carbon Footprint module. This gives you a realistic look at the methodology, data structure, and result quality – based on your own products.

mainEntity.name: Is a Product Carbon Footprint a one-time calculation?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Product Carbon Footprints evolve as data quality improves. Many companies start with initial calculations based on emission factor databases and refine their PCFs step by step with primary data from supply chains, production, or logistics. Global Changer supports this iterative approach. You can start quickly with initial PCFs and update them later with more accurate data.
